description: Implement a high-performance ETL pipeline using Oracle Pipelined Table Functions for the HR database (localhost:1521/XEPDB1, user: hr, pass: hr123). Create an object type T_IOT_METRIC (attributes: SENSOR_ID, READING_TIME, TEMP_CELSIUS, BATTERY_PCT, IS_LOW_BATTERY, STATUS) and a collection type T_IOT_METRIC_TAB. Develop a package PKG_IOT_ETL containing a pipelined function STREAM_METRICS that accepts a SYS_REFCURSOR and returns T_IOT_METRIC_TAB. The function must process data from RAW_SENSOR_LOGS (LOG_ID, RAW_PAYLOAD) where the payload format is SENSOR_ID|ISO_TIME|TEMP_F|BATTERY|STATUS. Transformations require converting ISO time to DATE, converting Fahrenheit to Celsius ((F-32)*5/9 rounded to 2 decimals), and setting IS_LOW_BATTERY to 'Y' if battery is under 20. If parsing fails, insert the LOG_ID and error message into IOT_PARSE_ERRORS and continue processing without raising an exception. Export the results of the function for all data in RAW_SENSOR_LOGS to /home/ga/Desktop/parsed_metrics.csv with headers.
